question: A FEEL condition calls `date and time("2024-06-31T10:00:00")` to parse a value coming from an external system. What does the call return?

options.text: `date and time("2024-07-01T10:00:00")`, automatically rolling over to the next valid day.
An incident, because parsing fails at the engine boundary.

a.text: The docs state that `date and time(from: string)` returns `null` when the string is not a valid calendar date, and call out exactly this case: `"2024-06-31T10:00:00"` is invalid because June only has 30 days.

b.text: The value is not returned as-is. The FEEL conversion function validates the calendar date and rejects June 31 because June has only 30 days.

c.text: FEEL does not auto-roll invalid calendar dates to the next valid day. Invalid input yields `null`, not a corrected value.

d.text: Invalid input to a FEEL built-in returns `null`; it does not raise an incident on its own. An incident only follows if downstream logic cannot cope with `null`.

explanation: `date and time(from: string)` parses the string and returns `null` whenever the string is not a valid calendar date. The docs use `"2024-06-31T10:00:00"` as the canonical example because June has only 30 days.
